body:
## Severity: LOW

## Game Affected
None of the supported games hit either boundary in vanilla content (Bethesda jumps over both 10.1.0.0 and 20.1.0.1). Pre-emptive correctness for hand-authored / modder NIFs at the exact boundary version, or future non-Bethesda Gamebryo content.

## Description

Source: AUDIT_NIF_2026-04-28.md (NIF-D3-11).

niftools' canonical `until=` semantics is **exclusive** — the field is present iff `version < until_version`. The current Rust gates use `version <= until_version`, which is one version too generous: at exactly `version == until_version` the field is read even though nif.xml says it's absent.

### Two known instances

**1. `NiTransformData::Order`** (`crates/nif/src/blocks/interpolator.rs:294`)

```rust
if stream.version() <= crate::version::NifVersion::V10_1_0_0 {
    let _order = stream.read_f32_le()?;
}
```

nif.xml line 4333:
```xml
<field name="Order" type="float" cond="Rotation Type == 4" until="10.1.0.0" />
```

→ field present iff `version < 10.1.0.0`. At v=10.1.0.0 the parser over-reads by 4 bytes (consumes the first 4 bytes of what should be the X-axis KeyGroup).

**2. `NiTexturingProperty::Apply Mode`** (`crates/nif/src/blocks/properties.rs:233`)

```rust
if stream.version() <= NifVersion(0x14010001) {
    let _apply_mode = stream.read_u32_le()?;
}
```

nif.xml:
```xml
<field name="Apply Mode" type="ApplyMode" since="3.3.0.13" until="20.1.0.1" />
```

→ field present iff `version < 20.1.0.1`. At v=20.1.0.1 the parser over-reads by 4 bytes (the four bytes that should have been `Texture Count`).

### Why this matters

The other half of the same pair — `since="N.M.O.P"` — is correctly inclusive (`version >= since`) in all sites I sampled. niflib's gen tool emits `if (version < to_version)` for `until` boundaries (verified via direct read of the niflib codegen template).

A follow-up reviewer hitting the same pattern in another nif.xml field could reasonably use the existing code as the precedent and propagate the bug.

## Impact

Zero today (no vanilla content lands at either boundary). Future risk: hand-authored / modder NIFs at exactly the boundary version drift the stream by 4 bytes per affected block; without `block_sizes` (Oblivion-era pre-10.1.0.0 hypothetical) the drift cascades for the rest of the file. With `block_sizes` (FO3+ hypothetical at v=20.1.0.1) the per-block recovery realigns, but the parsed struct silently misinterprets the reordered fields.

## Suggested Fix

Flip both `<=` to `<`. One-line change at each site. Add a regression test that builds a NIF at the exact boundary version and pins the byte count consumed.

After fixing, audit the rest of the parser via `grep -nE \"version\\(\\) <= NifVersion\\(0x\" crates/nif/src/` for any similar `until=` patterns that would benefit from the same flip.

## Related

- Audit: docs/audits/AUDIT_NIF_2026-04-28.md (NIF-D3-11)
- Prior fix: #714 (`33090a6`) added the `Order` read but used the wrong operator; see also `parse_transform_data_pre10_xyz_rotation_consumes_order_float` (uses 10.0.1.0, not the boundary)

## Completeness Checks

- [ ] **UNSAFE**: N/A
- [ ] **SIBLING**: Grep the parser for other `until=` mappings using `<=` and flip them in the same PR
- [ ] **DROP**: N/A
- [ ] **LOCK_ORDER**: N/A
- [ ] **FFI**: N/A
- [ ] **TESTS**: Boundary-exact regression for both sites (NIF at v=10.1.0.0 with NiTransformData XYZ rotation, NIF at v=20.1.0.1 with NiTexturingProperty)
title: NIF-D3-11: until="N.M.O.P" boundary uses <= not < — off-by-one on exact-version match
